MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-23-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LORNA WALKER, Esq. of Sweet & Walker, P.C. (Tel: 415-334-1600) for plaintiff;
Atty. OLGA VINER, Esq. of Gerard Fox Law P.C. (Tel: 310-441-0500) for defendant Vdopia, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT VDOPIA INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Vdopia Inc.'s CCP 1281.2 motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted. Plaintiff Starkingads Ltd. argues that no arbitration agreement exists. However, plaintiff attached the contract that contains the arbitration agreement to its complaint, incorporating it by reference and pleading breach of contract on it. Plaintiff also argues waiver, but defendant raised the issue of arbitration in April 2020. That was four months - one during a pandemic lockdown - after the case was filed. The parties are not "well into preparation" of the lawsuit and the initial trial date is in 2021. (See Burton v. Cruise (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 939, 945.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
